channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nancy Reagan's Childhood: From Instability to Elite Upbringing
item.description: Nancy Reagan's childhood was marked by two distinct phases. Born Anne Frances Robbins in 1921, her early years were unstable due to her father's absence and her mother's theatrical career. At age two,...

item.title: FIFA's Decision on Balogun Sparks Tuchel's Doubts Over Disciplinary Consistency
item.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel has raised concerns about FIFA's inconsistent disciplinary approach after United States striker Folarin Balogun was allowed to play despite receiving a red card during the ...

item.title: Woman Robbed in Broad Daylight Near Kenyatta Road Police Station
item.description: A woman was robbed and ambushed in broad daylight near a police station on Kenyatta Road, sparking concerns about insecurity in the area. The incident, which occurred on July 5, 2026, involved unknown...

item.title: UN Panel Discusses AI Governance in Annual Report Presentation
item.description: The United Nations will host a plenary session on July 6, 2026, featuring the annual report of the Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence. This event marks the opening o...

item.title: ICE's Internal Watchdog Investigates Online Critics Amid Free Speech Concerns
item.description: The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR), ICE's internal watchdog, has opened over 100 cases against individuals accused of 'doxing' and threatening ICE employees online. The controversy escala...

item.title: Legendary Concerts Remembered: A Collection of Iconic Performances
item.description: The article compiles personal recollections from writers about unforgettable live performances by iconic artists. It highlights moments such as Talking Heads' 1977 show at London's Rock Garden, where ...

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Former Teacher Dies After Cancer Battle as Singer Marries Travis Kelce
item.description: Kirk Schwabe, Taylor Swift's former criminal justice teacher and security guard, passed away at 69 after a battle with metastatic kidney cancer. His death occurred on the same day as Swift's wedding t...

item.title: Victorian State Election Poll Shows Tight Race Among Major Parties
item.description: With less than five months until the Victorian state election, a Redbridge poll reveals a closely contested race among major parties. One Nation leads with 27% of the primary vote, while Labor and the...

item.title: China faces regional criticism after intercontinental missile test
item.description: China conducted an intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) test from a submarine in the South Pacific on Monday, marking its first such launch in two years. The People’s Liberation Army Navy (PLAN) ...

item.title: Amjad Hussain Appointed as Chief Minister of Gilgit-Baltistan
item.description: Pakistan People’s Party (PPP) leader Amjad Hussain was sworn in as the chief minister of Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) on July 6, 2026, following his election on June 22. The ceremony, attended by PPP chairma...

item.title: Dalai Lama's 1955 Meeting with Mao Zedong Reveals Concerns Over Tibet's Future
item.description: The article details a pivotal meeting between the 14th Dalai Lama and Chairman Mao Zedong in 1955, which marked a turning point in the Dalai Lama's understanding of the threats facing Tibet. During th...

item.title: Sudan Conflict Escalates as UN Warns of Humanitarian Crisis; Nigeria and Mali Face Food and Security Challenges
item.description: The United Nations has raised alarms about the escalating Sudan conflict, with El Obeid under relentless drone attacks that have killed at least 45 civilians and destroyed critical infrastructure. UN ...

item.title: The Role of Therapy in Entrepreneurial Success
item.description: The article emphasizes the critical need for entrepreneurs, especially in East Africa, to address personal emotional and psychological challenges that impact business leadership and decision-making. I...

item.title: Commerce Secretary Lutnick Faces Backlash Over Trump Gold Card Amid Epstein Ties Resurfacing
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick faced intense public backlash after sharing an image of the Trump Gold Card, a $1 million–$5 million visa program for wealthy immigrants, on social media. The post re...

item.title: Balancing Travel and Financial Goals: Strategies for Canadians
item.description: The article explores how couples like Patrice and Isabelle, a Burlington, Ont., couple in their 30s, can pursue travel aspirations without compromising financial stability. Financial planner Lara Maka...

item.title: Tanzania–China consider pilot 10-day visa-free transit arrangement for business travellers in transit
item.description: Tanzania and China are currently in discussions over a proposed pilot arrangement that would allow eligible Tanzanian travellers transiting through China to third countries to enter China without a vi...

item.title: SpaceX Set to Join NASDAQ-100 Index
item.description: The NASDAQ-100 index is set to include SpaceX, marking a significant milestone for the aerospace company. This inclusion reflects SpaceX's growing influence in the technology and stock markets. The de...

item.title: Iran mourns assassinated supreme leader with massive funeral procession
item.description: The funeral procession of Iran's assassinated supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began in Tehran, drawing millions of mourners. Authorities estimated the turnout to rival the numbers from his prede...
